differential_diagnoses: Occult solid tumor (e.g., lung or pancreatic carcinoma)
next_steps: Bone marrow aspiration and biopsy with cytogenetics and flow cytometry
Serum soluble IL-2 receptor level and NK cell activity (HLH-2004 criteria workup)
CT of the chest with contrast to evaluate lung nodules and mediastinal lymphadenopathy
Consider PET/CT scan to identify occult malignancy sites
Diagnostic paracentesis or ultrasound-guided biopsy of suspicious hepatic/lung lesions if safe
Supportive care with transfusion for severe cytopenias and management of pancreatitis
